ice.c(3) Library Functions Manual ice.c(3)

NAME

ice.c - ICE candidate gathering and exchange.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define PCRE2_CODE_UNIT_WIDTH 8

Functions


const char * ice_candidate_type_name (ice_candidate_type_t type)
const char * ice_protocol_name (ice_protocol_t protocol)
uint32_t ice_calculate_priority (ice_candidate_type_t type, uint16_t local_preference, uint8_t component_id)
uint32_t ice_calculate_priority_for_candidate (const ice_candidate_t *candidate)
asciichat_error_t ice_parse_candidate (const char *line, ice_candidate_t *candidate)
asciichat_error_t ice_format_candidate (const ice_candidate_t *candidate, char *line, size_t line_size)
asciichat_error_t ice_gather_candidates (const ice_config_t *config)
asciichat_error_t ice_add_remote_candidate (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, const ice_candidate_t *candidate, const char *mid)
bool ice_is_connected (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc)
asciichat_error_t ice_get_selected_pair_impl (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, ice_candidate_t *local_candidate, ice_candidate_t *remote_candidate)
Get selected ICE candidate pair (C++ implementation). asciichat_error_t ice_get_selected_pair (webrtc_peer_connection_t *pc, ice_candidate_t *local_candidate, ice_candidate_t *remote_candidate)

Detailed Description

ICE candidate gathering and exchange.

Implements ICE candidate collection from STUN/TURN servers and relay via ACDS signaling.

Date

January 2026

Definition in file ice.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define PCRE2_CODE_UNIT_WIDTH 8

Definition at line 19 of file ice.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t ice_add_remote_candidate (webrtc_peer_connection_t * pc, const ice_candidate_t * candidate, const char * mid)

Definition at line 422 of file ice.c.

423                                                             {
424   if (!pc || !candidate || !mid) {
425     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid remote candidate parameters");
426   }
427 
428   // Validate candidate has required fields
429   if (candidate->ip_address[0] == '\0' || candidate->port == 0) {
430     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Candidate missing IP address or port");
431   }
432 
433   log_debug("ICE: Adding remote candidate %s:%u (type=%s, foundation=%s) on mid=%s", candidate->ip_address,
434             candidate->port, ice_candidate_type_name(candidate->type), candidate->foundation, mid);
435 
436   // Format candidate to SDP attribute line for libdatachannel
437   char candidate_line[512];
438   asciichat_error_t err = ice_format_candidate(candidate, candidate_line, sizeof(candidate_line));
439   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
440     return err;
441   }
442 
443   // Delegate to WebRTC layer
444   return webrtc_add_remote_candidate(pc, candidate_line, mid);
445 }




References ice_candidate_type_name(),
    ice_format_candidate(), and webrtc_add_remote_candidate().






uint32_t
  ice_calculate_priority (ice_candidate_type_t type, uint16_t local_preference,
  uint8_t component_id)


Definition at line 101 of file ice.c.


101                                                                                                             {
102   // RFC 5245: priority = (2^24 * typePreference) + (2^8 * localPreference) + (256 - componentID)
103 
104   // Type preference (higher is better):
105   // - Host: 126 (most preferred, lowest latency)
106   // - SRFLX: 100 (STUN-discovered address)
107   // - PRFLX: 110 (discovered during connectivity checks)
108   // - Relay: 0 (least preferred, uses bandwidth)
109 
110   uint8_t type_preference = 0;
111   switch (type) {
112   case ICE_CANDIDATE_HOST:
113     type_preference = 126;
114     break;
115   case ICE_CANDIDATE_SRFLX:
116     type_preference = 100;
117     break;
118   case ICE_CANDIDATE_PRFLX:
119     type_preference = 110;
120     break;
121   case ICE_CANDIDATE_RELAY:
122     type_preference = 0;
123     break;
124   default:
125     type_preference = 0;
126     break;
127   }
128 
129   uint32_t priority =
130       (((uint32_t)type_preference) << 24) | (((uint32_t)local_preference) << 8) | (256 - (uint32_t)component_id);
131 
132   return priority;
133 }






Referenced by ice_calculate_priority_for_candidate().






uint32_t
  ice_calculate_priority_for_candidate (const ice_candidate_t *
  candidate)


Definition at line 135 of file ice.c.


135                                                                                 {
136   if (!candidate) {
137     return 0;
138   }
139 
140   // Calculate local preference based on IP classification
141   uint16_t local_pref = ice_calculate_local_preference_by_ip(candidate->ip_address);
142 
143   // Use standard RFC 5245 priority formula with IP-aware local preference
144   return ice_calculate_priority(candidate->type, local_pref, (uint8_t)candidate->component_id);
145 }






References ice_calculate_priority().






const
  char * ice_candidate_type_name (ice_candidate_type_t type)


Definition at line 26 of file ice.c.


26                                                                {
27   switch (type) {
28   case ICE_CANDIDATE_HOST:
29     return "host";
30   case ICE_CANDIDATE_SRFLX:
31     return "srflx";
32   case ICE_CANDIDATE_PRFLX:
33     return "prflx";
34   case ICE_CANDIDATE_RELAY:
35     return "relay";
36   default:
37     return "unknown";
38   }
39 }

    ice_format_candidate().






asciichat_error_t
  ice_format_candidate (const ice_candidate_t * candidate, char * line, size_t
  line_size)


Definition at line 322 of file ice.c.


322                                                                                                        {
323   if (!candidate || !line || line_size == 0) {
324     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid candidate format parameters");
325   }
326 
327   // Format: foundation component protocol priority ip port typ type [raddr rport] [tcptype type]
328   // Example: 1 1 udp 2130706431 192.168.1.1 54321 typ host
329   // Example with raddr: 1 1 udp 2130706431 203.0.113.45 54321 typ srflx raddr 192.168.1.1 rport 12345
330 
331   int written = safe_snprintf(line, line_size, "%s %u %s %u %s %u typ %s", candidate->foundation,
332                               candidate->component_id, ice_protocol_name(candidate->protocol), candidate->priority,
333                               candidate->ip_address, candidate->port, ice_candidate_type_name(candidate->type));
334 
335   if (written < 0 || (size_t)written >= line_size) {
336     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_FULL, "Candidate line too large for buffer");
337   }
338 
339   size_t remaining = line_size - (size_t)written;
340   char *pos = line + written;
341 
342   // 2. If srflx/prflx/relay: append raddr and rport
343   if (candidate->type == ICE_CANDIDATE_SRFLX || candidate->type == ICE_CANDIDATE_PRFLX ||
344       candidate->type == ICE_CANDIDATE_RELAY) {
345     if (candidate->raddr[0] != '\0') {
346       int appended = safe_snprintf(pos, remaining, " raddr %s rport %u", candidate->raddr, candidate->rport);
347       if (appended < 0 || (size_t)appended >= remaining) {
348         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_FULL, "Cannot append raddr/rport to candidate line");
349       }
350       pos += appended;
351       remaining -= (size_t)appended;
352     }
353   }
354 
355   // 3. If TCP: append tcptype
356   if (candidate->protocol == ICE_PROTOCOL_TCP) {
357     const char *tcptype_str = "passive"; // default
358     if (candidate->tcp_type == ICE_TCP_TYPE_ACTIVE) {
359       tcptype_str = "active";
360     } else if (candidate->tcp_type == ICE_TCP_TYPE_SO) {
361       tcptype_str = "so";
362     }
363 
364     int appended = safe_snprintf(pos, remaining, " tcptype %s", tcptype_str);
365     if (appended < 0 || (size_t)appended >= remaining) {
366       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_FULL, "Cannot append tcptype to candidate line");
367     }
368     pos += appended;
369     remaining -= (size_t)appended;
370   }
371 
372   // 4. Append any extensions
373   if (candidate->extensions[0] != '\0') {
374     int appended = safe_snprintf(pos, remaining, " %s", candidate->extensions);
375     if (appended < 0 || (size_t)appended >= remaining) {
376       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_FULL, "Cannot append extensions to candidate line");
377     }
378   }
379 
380   // 5. Ensure line is null-terminated (snprintf already does this)
381   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
382 }






References ice_candidate_type_name(),
    ice_protocol_name(), and safe_snprintf().


Referenced by ice_add_remote_candidate().






asciichat_error_t
  ice_gather_candidates (const ice_config_t * config)


Definition at line 388 of file ice.c.


388                                                                     {
389   if (!config) {
390     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid ICE config");
391   }
392 
393   if (!config->send_callback) {
394     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ICE config missing send_callback");
395   }
396 
397   log_debug("ICE: Starting candidate gathering (ufrag=%s, pwd=%s)", config->ufrag, config->pwd);
398 
399   // NOTE: libdatachannel (juice) handles ICE candidate gathering internally.
400   // This function is a placeholder for the callback-based API.
401   // The actual gathering happens in the peer_manager when you:
402   //   1. Create peer connection with rtcCreatePeerConnection()
403   //   2. Set ice candidate callback with rtcSetIceCandidateCallback()
404   //   3. libdatachannel automatically gathers candidates from:
405   //      - Local interfaces (host candidates)
406   //      - STUN servers (server-reflexive candidates)
407   //      - TURN servers (relay candidates)
408   //   4. When a candidate is found, rtcSetIceCandidateCallback() is called
409   //   5. Application (webrtc.c) formats and sends via ACDS signaling
410   //
411   // This ice_gather_candidates() function may not be called directly -
412   // the gathering happens automatically when peer connection is created.
413   // Keeping this for API compatibility.
414 
415   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
416 }










asciichat_error_t
  ice_get_selected_pair (webrtc_peer_connection_t * pc, ice_candidate_t *
  local_candidate, ice_candidate_t * remote_candidate)


Definition at line 461 of file ice.c.


462                                                                            {
463   // Delegate to C++ implementation which uses libdatachannel's C++ API
464   return ice_get_selected_pair_impl(pc, local_candidate, remote_candidate);
465 }






References ice_get_selected_pair_impl().






asciichat_error_t
  ice_get_selected_pair_impl (webrtc_peer_connection_t * pc, ice_candidate_t *
  local_candidate, ice_candidate_t * remote_candidate)


Get selected ICE candidate pair (C++ implementation). This
    function uses libdatachannel's C++ API to retrieve the selected candidate
    pair, which is not available in the C API.


Definition at line 58 of file
  ice_selected_pair.cpp.


59                                                                                 {
60   if (!pc) {
61     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
62   }
63 
64   try {
65     // Get the libdatachannel peer connection ID using helper function
66     int rtc_id = webrtc_get_rtc_id(pc);
67     if (rtc_id < 0) {
68       return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
69     }
70 
71     // Query selected candidate pair from libdatachannel
72     // Note: This requires libdatachannel to have the selected candidate pair available
73     char local_buf[512];
74     char remote_buf[512];
75 
76     // Try to get the selected local candidate
77     if (rtcGetSelectedCandidatePair(rtc_id, local_buf, sizeof(local_buf), remote_buf, sizeof(remote_buf)) < 0) {
78       // No pair selected yet, or error accessing it
79       return ERROR_INVALID_STATE;
80     }
81 
82     // Parse local candidate if requested
83     if (local_candidate) {
84       std::string local_str(local_buf);
85       asciichat_error_t err = parse_datachannel_candidate(local_str, local_candidate);
86       if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
87         return err;
88       }
89     }
90 
91     // Parse remote candidate if requested
92     if (remote_candidate) {
93       std::string remote_str(remote_buf);
94       asciichat_error_t err = parse_datachannel_candidate(remote_str, remote_candidate);
95       if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
96         return err;
97       }
98     }
99 
100     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
101 
102   } catch (const std::exception &e) {
103     return ERROR_NETWORK;
104   } catch (...) {
105     return ERROR_NETWORK;
106   }
107 }






References webrtc_get_rtc_id().


Referenced by ice_get_selected_pair().






bool ice_is_connected (webrtc_peer_connection_t * pc)


Definition at line 447 of file ice.c.


447                                                     {
448   if (!pc) {
449     return false;
450   }
451 
452   // Check peer connection state
453   webrtc_state_t state = webrtc_get_state(pc);
454   return (state == WEBRTC_STATE_CONNECTED);
455 }
